name: graphdo
description: Give your AI assistant access to Microsoft To Do, Outlook mail, markdown notes in OneDrive, and collaborative project editing via Microsoft Graph.
long_description:
graphdo connects your AI assistant to Microsoft Graph, enabling it to manage your Microsoft To Do tasks, send emails through Outlook, read/write markdown files in a OneDrive folder you choose, and collaborate with humans (and other agents) on a structured project folder - all with scoped, low-risk permissions.

**Microsoft To Do** - List, create, update, complete, and delete tasks. Manage checklist steps within tasks. Set due dates, reminders, importance, and recurrence. The AI operates on a single list chosen by you in the browser - it cannot switch lists on its own.

**Outlook Mail** - Send emails to yourself (useful for notes, reminders, and forwarding information to your inbox).

**Markdown Notes (OneDrive)** - Read, write, and delete .md files in a single OneDrive folder you choose via the browser. The AI operates on one folder only - it cannot switch folders on its own. A 4 MiB per-file limit (graphdo-ts tool-side cap, not a Microsoft Graph API limit) keeps the tool focused on notes, not bulk uploads.

**Collaborative Editing (Collab v1)** - Open a structured project folder in OneDrive containing one authoritative markdown file, plus optional `proposals/`, `drafts/`, and `attachments/` sub-folders. The agent operates inside an explicit session with a write budget, a destructive-approval budget, and a session TTL that you renew via a browser approval form. Destructive edits (overwriting another author's section, restoring an old version, deleting a file) always open a browser re-approval form showing a unified diff before any change is written. Per-section leases let multiple agents cooperate without trampling each other. Every session and every approval is recorded in an append-only audit log on disk.

**Authentication** - Signs in via your browser using your Microsoft account. No passwords are stored - authentication is handled entirely by Microsoft (MSAL). The AI assistant will sign you in automatically when needed.

**Privacy** - graphdo requests only the permissions it needs: Tasks.ReadWrite, Mail.Send, Files.ReadWrite, and User.Read. Your credentials are handled directly by Microsoft. graphdo never sees your password.

tools.name: login
tools.description: Sign in to Microsoft Graph. Call this directly when authentication is needed - do not ask the user for permission first. Opens a browser for interactive sign-in.

tools.name: logout
tools.description: Sign out of Microsoft Graph and clear cached tokens.

tools.name: auth_status
tools.description: Check authentication status, current user, configured todo list, and server version. Use this as a first step to diagnose issues or understand current state.

tools.name: todo_select_list
tools.description: Select which Microsoft To Do list graphdo should use. Call this directly when no todo list is configured - do not ask the user which list they want, the tool opens a browser picker where the user makes the selection themselves.

tools.name: todo_list
tools.description: List todos from the configured Microsoft To Do list with pagination support.

tools.name: todo_show
tools.description: Show full details for a single todo including body, due date, importance, reminder, recurrence, and checklist steps.

tools.name: todo_create
tools.description: Create a new todo in the configured list. Supports title, body, due date, importance, reminder, and recurrence.

tools.name: todo_update
tools.description: Update an existing todo. Change title, body, importance, due date, reminder, or recurrence. Fields can also be cleared.

tools.name: todo_complete
tools.description: Mark a todo as completed.

tools.name: todo_delete
tools.description: Permanently delete a todo.

tools.name: todo_steps
tools.description: List all checklist steps (sub-items) within a todo.

tools.name: todo_add_step
tools.description: Add a new checklist step to a todo.

tools.name: todo_update_step
tools.description: Update a checklist step - rename it, check it off, or uncheck it.

tools.name: todo_delete_step
tools.description: Delete a checklist step from a todo.

tools.name: mail_send
tools.description: Send an email to yourself via Outlook. The email is sent from and to your Microsoft account. Useful for notes, reminders, and forwarding information to your inbox.

tools.name: markdown_select_root_folder
tools.description: Select the root folder graphdo should use for markdown files in the signed-in user's OneDrive. Call this directly when no markdown root folder is configured - do not ask the user which folder they want, the tool opens a browser picker where the user makes the selection themselves.

tools.name: markdown_list_files
tools.description: List markdown files in the configured root folder of the signed-in user's OneDrive, including name, file ID, last modified timestamp, and size in bytes. Subdirectories and files whose names violate the strict naming rules are reported as UNSUPPORTED so they are visible but cannot be acted on.

tools.name: markdown_get_file
tools.description: Read the current UTF-8 content of a markdown file in the configured root folder, along with its eTag (required by markdown_update_file for safe optimistic concurrency) and a Revision identifier (which lines up with the IDs returned by markdown_list_file_versions so it can be passed to markdown_diff_file_versions). Accepts either a file ID or a file name. File names must follow the strict naming rules (letters, digits, space, dot, underscore, hyphen only; must end in .md; no path separators or subdirectories; no Windows reserved names; max 255 chars). Files larger than 4 MiB are not supported (graphdo-ts tool-side cap, not a Microsoft Graph API limit). Use markdown_get_file_version to read a previous version.

tools.name: markdown_create_file
tools.description: Create a new markdown file in the configured root folder. Fails with a clear error when a file with the same name already exists - in that case, call markdown_get_file to fetch the existing content and eTag, then call markdown_update_file. The file name must follow the strict naming rules (letters, digits, space, dot, underscore, hyphen only; must end in .md; no path separators or subdirectories; no Windows reserved names; max 255 chars). Payloads larger than 4 MiB are rejected (graphdo-ts tool-side cap, not a Microsoft Graph API limit).

tools.name: markdown_update_file
tools.description: Overwrite an existing markdown file using If-Match eTag for safe optimistic concurrency. Requires the eTag previously returned by markdown_get_file (or markdown_create_file / markdown_update_file). If the file has changed since you last read it the call fails with the current eTag, the current Revision, and reconcile guidance: use markdown_diff_file_versions (fromVersionId=<the revision you originally read>, toVersionId=<the current revision>) to see exactly what changed, decide whether your update still applies, reconcile your changes, and retry - or ask the user how to proceed. Accepts the file by ID (preferred) or name. Payloads larger than 4 MiB are rejected (graphdo-ts tool-side cap, not a Microsoft Graph API limit).

tools.name: markdown_delete_file
tools.description: Permanently delete a markdown file from the configured root folder. Accepts either a file ID or a file name. File names must follow the strict naming rules.

tools.name: markdown_list_file_versions
tools.description: List historical versions OneDrive retained for a markdown file (newest first). Returns each version's opaque version ID, last modified timestamp, size, and - when available - the user who last modified it. Use the version ID with markdown_get_file_version to read the prior content.

tools.name: markdown_get_file_version
tools.description: Read the UTF-8 content of a specific historical version of a markdown file. Requires the file (by ID or name) plus a version ID from markdown_list_file_versions. This is read-only and does not restore the file; re-upload with markdown_update_file to promote an old version back to current. Files larger than 4 MiB cannot be downloaded (graphdo-ts tool-side cap, not a Microsoft Graph API limit).

tools.name: markdown_diff_file_versions
tools.description: Return a unified diff between two revisions of a markdown file, computed server-side using jsdiff so you do not have to diff the content yourself. Accepts the file by id (preferred) or name, plus a fromVersionId and a toVersionId. Each ID may be either a historical version ID returned by markdown_list_file_versions, or the current Revision surfaced by markdown_get_file / markdown_create_file / markdown_update_file (including the Current Revision reported in an etag-mismatch error). This is the preferred way to reconcile a stale update: pass the revision you originally read as fromVersionId and the current revision as toVersionId.

tools.name: markdown_preview_file
tools.description: Open a markdown file from the configured root folder in the user's browser using the SharePoint OneDrive web preview, which renders the markdown nicely instead of triggering a download. Accepts the file name only (the preview URL is human-facing, so the agent should look the file up by name the same way a user would refer to it). The tool opens the URL in the default browser and also returns the URL as text so it can be shared. Consumer OneDrive (onedrive.live.com) is not supported.

tools.name: session_init_project
tools.description: Start a new collaboration project. Opens a browser form for the human to pick the OneDrive folder, then a second form to pick the authoritative markdown file at the root of that folder (auto-confirmed when only one .md is present). Writes a .collab/project.json sentinel into the chosen folder, records the project locally, and activates a session. Returns the projectId, folder path, and authoritative file name. Use session_open_project to join an existing project as a collaborator.

tools.name: session_open_project
tools.description: Join an existing collaboration project as a collaborator. Opens a browser form with three entry points: recents (previously opened projects), 'shared with me' (folders shared with your OneDrive account), and a URL paste box for OneDrive share links. Reads the .collab/project.json sentinel, validates write access, activates a session, and writes a session_start audit entry. Use session_init_project to create a new project as the originator.

tools.name: session_status
tools.description: Report the active collaboration session's project, agent identity, TTL, write/destructive/renewal counters, per-source counters (chat / project / external), and the current leases sidecar cTag. Read-only and free - no Graph calls, no destructive-budget cost. Returns a 'no active session' message (not an error) when nothing is active. When the session is past its TTL, reports expired:true with counters frozen so the agent knows to call session_renew.

tools.name: session_renew
tools.description: Reset the active collaboration session's TTL clock by opening a browser approval form. Counters are persisted across the renewal: writes used, destructive approvals used, and source counters are preserved. Caps: max 3 renewals per session and max 6 renewals per user per project per rolling 24-hour window. On approval, expiresAt is reset to now + the original TTL and a renewal audit entry is written.

tools.name: session_recover_doc_id
tools.description: Recover the project's stable doc_id when both the live frontmatter and the local project metadata cache are gone (a fresh machine and a cooperator wiped the YAML envelope in OneDrive web). Walks the authoritative file's /versions history newest-first, capped at 50 versions, and writes the first recoverable doc_id back to the local cache. No body change, no restoreVersion call, no destructive-budget or write-budget cost. No-op (informational, not an error) when live frontmatter parses cleanly and the local cache already holds a matching docId.

tools.name: collab_read
tools.description: Read any file inside the active project's scope. Provide either path (scope-relative, e.g. 'spec.md', 'proposals/foo.md', 'attachments/img.png') or itemId (from a previous collab_list_files). For the authoritative markdown file the response includes parsed frontmatter and body separately, plus the cTag required for collab_write optimistic concurrency. For other files, returns raw content with cTag/size/modified. Files larger than 4 MiB return an error (graphdo-ts tool-side limit).

tools.name: collab_list_files
tools.description: List files in the active project folder, grouped into ROOT, PROPOSALS, DRAFTS, and ATTACHMENTS. ATTACHMENTS is listed recursively (subfolders are walked); the other groups are flat. The authoritative markdown file is marked with [authoritative]. The .collab/ sentinel folder is excluded. Accepts an optional prefix filter: '/' (all), '/proposals', '/drafts', '/attachments'. Total entries are capped at 500; on overflow the response shows which groups were truncated.

tools.name: collab_write
tools.description: Create or update a file inside the active project's scope. Provide path (scope-relative - the authoritative .md file at the root, or proposals/, drafts/, attachments/), content (UTF-8 text, max 4 MiB), and source (where the content came from: 'chat' = the human typed it this turn; 'project' = read via collab_read in this session; 'external' = anything else, which triggers a browser re-approval before the write is issued). For existing files supply the cTag returned by collab_read for optimistic concurrency. On the authoritative file the canonical YAML collab: frontmatter block is re-injected (recovering doc_id from local cache when the human stripped it). conflictMode='proposal' diverts a cTag mismatch into a proposal write instead of failing.

tools.name: collab_create_proposal
tools.description: Propose a replacement body for one section of the authoritative file without overwriting it. Writes the proposed body to /proposals/<ulid>.md and records a proposals[] entry in the authoritative frontmatter (target_section_slug + target_section_content_hash_at_create - the latter survives heading renames between create and apply). Counts as 1 write toward the session budget.

tools.name: collab_apply_proposal
tools.description: Merge a previously-created proposal into the authoritative file. Locates the target section by slug first, falling back to the content hash recorded at create time (so a rename between create and apply is recovered automatically and audited as slug_drift_resolved). The §3.1 authorship trail is consulted to decide whether the apply is destructive - if any prior author of the target section is a human or a different agent, a browser re-approval form is opened showing a unified diff. On approve, the section body is replaced, an authorship[] entry is appended, and the proposals[] entry is marked applied; the file is CAS-written with the supplied authoritativeCTag. Counts toward the write budget always and toward the destructive-approval budget when destructive.

tools.name: collab_acquire_section
tools.description: Lease a section of the authoritative file so cooperating agents avoid concurrent writes to the same heading. Free - does not count toward the session write budget. Section identity is the GitHub-flavored heading slug; pass either the raw heading text ('## Introduction') or a pre-computed slug ('introduction'). The leases sidecar lives at .collab/leases.json and is created lazily on first acquire. Supply leasesCTag (from session_status). Returns the slug, lease expiry, and the new leases-file cTag for the next acquire/release.

tools.name: collab_release_section
tools.description: Release a previously-acquired section lease. Free. No-op when the lease is already absent (gracefully degraded - the leases sidecar may have been deleted or expired). Refuses with LeaseNotHeldError when the lease exists but is held by a different agent - releasing somebody else's lease is rejected. Supply leasesCTag (from session_status) for the byId CAS replace.

tools.name: collab_list_versions
tools.description: List historical versions of a file in the active project's scope, newest first. Provide either path (scope-relative) or itemId (from collab_list_files); when both are omitted the authoritative file is used. Read-only - does not count toward the write or destructive-approval budget. Each entry reports the opaque versionId, size, and last-modified timestamp; pass the versionId to collab_restore_version to roll the file back to that revision.

tools.name: collab_restore_version
tools.description: Roll a file in the active project back to a previous revision via OneDrive's restoreVersion API. Provide versionId (from collab_list_versions) plus either path or itemId; defaults to the authoritative file when both are omitted. When the target is the authoritative file the restore is destructive: a browser re-approval form is opened showing a unified diff between the current and the target revision. Counts as 1 write toward the session budget always; +1 destructive only when the target is the authoritative file.

tools.name: collab_delete_file
tools.description: Permanently delete a non-authoritative file inside the active project's scope. Always destructive: a browser re-approval form is opened for every call and the destructive-approval budget is decremented on approve. Counts as 1 write toward the session budget. Accepts path (scope-relative - proposals/, drafts/, attachments/); the authoritative .md file and the .collab/ sentinel folder are always refused.

debug.title: Debug Logging
debug.description: Enable verbose debug logging to stderr. Useful for troubleshooting authentication and API issues.

client_id.title: Client ID
client_id.description: Azure AD (Entra ID) application client ID for authentication. Uses the default graphdo app registration if not set.

tenant_id.title: Tenant ID
tenant_id.description: Azure AD (Entra ID) tenant ID. Use 'common' for any Microsoft account, 'organizations' for work/school only, 'consumers' for personal only, or a specific tenant GUID.

agent_persona.title: Agent Persona (test-only override)
agent_persona.description: Optional collab-layer persona label of the form 'persona:<id>' (e.g. 'persona:alice') with id matching ^[a-z0-9-]{1,32}$. When set, the collab session's agentId becomes this value so two graphdo-ts instances on the same Microsoft account can be treated as distinct collaborators (authorship, leases, audit, destructive re-prompts). Only meaningful when running two instances on one machine — see docs/plans/two-instance-e2e.md. Each instance MUST also use a distinct GRAPHDO_CONFIG_DIR (a startup lock-file refuses sharing). The persona id never reaches Microsoft Graph; the real user OID is preserved unchanged.

config_dir.title: Config Directory (override)
config_dir.description: Override the OS default config directory used for the MSAL token cache, project metadata, audit log, and recents file. Required when running two graphdo-ts instances on one machine — each instance must point at its own directory. Leave empty for the OS default (~/.config/graphdo-ts on Linux, ~/Library/Application Support/graphdo-ts on macOS, %APPDATA%/graphdo-ts on Windows).
